title: Interim Clinical Considerations for Use of Vaccine for Monkeypox Prevention in the United States | Monkeypox | CDC
text:

JYNNEOS has been studied as a 2-dose, double dose, and 3-dose series in patients with HIV, including those with low (<200 cells/mm 3 ) CD4 counts. There was no significant difference in seroconversion rates with the addition of the third dose or by doubling the dose, with similar seroconversion rates across groups. Additional doses were discussed in the June 2023 meeting of the U.S. Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) , and the ACIP did not believe the available data supported an additional dose in people who are immunocompromised. Therefore, CDC does not recommend additional doses of vaccine for that population. 
CDC will continue to monitor JYNNEOS vaccination data from these and other studies.
Evaluating patients 
Evaluating and counseling patients who could benefit from monkeypox vaccination is critical. To do so, healthcare professionals should perform a comprehensive social and sexual history .
Attention should be paid to sexual behaviors (e.g. frequency and types of sex) and partners (e.g. number and frequency), due to the populations that have been disproportionately affected by the ongoing clade II monkeypox outbreak.
